description:
You are acting as a compliance officer who has been asked to collect a very small, **three–line** audit snapshot of an existing Git repository that lives at

    /home/user/compliance_repo

Inside that repository there is already one commit and an “origin” remote configured.  
Your job is to produce a single text-file report that captures three critical data points:

1. The short (7-character) hash of the most recent commit.  
2. The author’s full name for that commit.  
3. The URL of the “origin” remote.

Requirements for the audit report:

• The report **must** be saved as  
  /home/user/compliance_audit.log

• The file must contain exactly three lines—nothing before the first line and nothing after the third line.

• Each line must follow **precisely** this key=value format (case-sensitive, no extra spaces):

    Commit=<SHORT_HASH>
    Author=<AUTHOR_NAME>
    Remote=<ORIGIN_URL>

• <SHORT_HASH> must be the 7-character hash returned by  
  `git rev-parse --short HEAD` inside /home/user/compliance_repo.  
• <AUTHOR_NAME> must be the exact author string produced by  
  `git log -1 --pretty=format:%an` inside that repo.  
• <ORIGIN_URL> must be the exact URL shown by  
  `git remote get-url origin`.

Hints (do NOT print these in your final output):

• You do not need to create or modify the repository—only read from it.  
• Only 2–3 Git commands are required.  
• Ensure the final log file is created at the correct absolute path with the exact three lines and no trailing blank line; automated tests will fail otherwise.

Once the file is in place, your task is complete. You do not need to show any command output in the terminal; only the existence and correctness of /home/user/compliance_audit.log will be checked.
truth:
Repository path: /home/user/compliance_repo  

State before the agent starts:
• It is already a valid Git repository.
• It contains one commit whose full 40-character SHA-1 is  
  51f2c3d7b991c7aa0123456789abcdefabcde999
• The 7-character short hash is  
  51f2c3d
• The commit’s author name is  
  Sam Auditor
• The commit’s author email is  
  audit@example.com
• Commit date is irrelevant for the test.
• Commit message is “Add initial policy”.
• The commit contains a single tracked file, /home/user/compliance_repo/policy.txt, whose content is:
  All staff must follow company compliance policy.

Remote configuration:
• There is a single remote named “origin” whose URL is  
  https://example.com/internal/compliance_repo.git

Expected file created by the agent:
/home/user/compliance_audit.log  (plain text, permissions 644 is sufficient)

Exact required content (no leading or trailing whitespace, each line terminated by a single newline, no extra newline at the end of the file):

Commit=51f2c3d
Author=Sam Auditor
Remote=https://example.com/internal/compliance_repo.git
name: task_000000_701e810c
